channel.title: Vault
channel.generator: Webpage HTML Export plugin for Obsidian

item.title: Manufacturing Implementation Plan
item.description:
Background info (full) **2025 PRESENTATION SCENARIO/PROMPT:**For 2026, Baja SAE will use the following Business Presentation prompt: Outdoor Adventure Holdings is a property management company that recently purchased a 600,000-acre ranch in Montana, USA. Our plan is to develop the ranch to include a large resort/hotel, ski lift area, and build lakefront infrastructure for boating activities during the summer. The resort would support various outdoor activities like mountain biking, fishing, hiking trails, as well as managing a herd of cattle and other livestock. The property is meant to be an immersive experience for the guests who visit. Outdoor Adventure Holdings is seeking a vehicle to meet their needs for providing support for all the previously mentioned activities. The vehicles would be used heavily and a high level of operation without maintenance issues would be expected. Outdoor Adventure Holdings would like to test drive some of the vehicles prior to signing a purchase agreement. Outdoor Adventure Holdings initial request would be for 20 vehicles to be provided six months from breaking ground to support the construction phase of the project. This would save overall costs by eliminating rentals. Once the construction phase is underway (18 months after breaking ground) another 50 units would be needed. These would be long term support vehicles for daily operations. We also request information on what infrastructure would be needed to support the vehicles in terms of storage, maintenance, spare parts, fueling etc. Outdoor Adventure Holdings is seeking a proposal for the construction support vehicle purchase as well as the long-term daily use vehicles. Our firm would like to understand how your company will support our goals of long-term profitability with minimal downtime and overhead operating costs. An additional opportunity exists to develop part of the property into UTV trails. Outdoor Adventure Holdings is looking to see how this proposed land use could be marketed. Round 1 is a written Request for Proposal (RFP) response. Your task in Round 1 is to convince Outdoor Adventure Holdings your vehicle design and manufacturing capability will meet their product demands. Successful completion will earn you a virtual presentation slot for Round 2. Round 2 is a virtual, ten-minute presentation to a board of Outdoor Adventure Holdings product specialists to ultimately choose a single company to contract for design and manufacturing of a vehicle. Your RFP response and live presentations should include, but is not limited to: Snapshot of your company including (but not limited to) location, size, market share, employee diversity, past product designs and financial performance Cost projections and timelines around purchased parts, manufacturing layout, and production goals for ongoing manufacturing should the design be selected Proposal for prototype testing/validation of vehicle design to meet client needs Marketing plan to acquire materials and manufacturing to facilitate robust, but cost effective solutions and customer growth opportunities The company Outdoor Adventure Holdings is looking for a company to supply work and recreation vehicles for their ATV Ranch. The requirements are summarized as follows: 20 initial cars in 6 months (before construction starts) Thus 4 cars/month min to meet requirement, with the ability to be short on 4 cars and still meet deadline.
50 post construction cars in 18 months (before construction ends) Thus 3 cars/month min to meet requirement, with the ability to be short on 4 cars and still meet deadline. These would be long term support vehicles for daily operations. We also request information on what infrastructure would be needed to support the vehicles in terms of: storage maintenance spare parts fueling .... Outdoor Adventure Holdings is seeking a proposal for the construction support vehicle purchase as well as the long-term daily use vehicles. Our firm would like to understand how your company will support our goals of long-term profitability with minimal downtime and overhead operating costs. An additional opportunity exists to develop part of the property into UTV trails. Outdoor Adventure Holdings is looking to see how this proposed land use could be marketed. Optimization goals: Base the output around the previously stated number of "employees" and assume they work normal hours (so everyone is working in same area at same time -> need more space than minimum output) Significant capability for expansion to something larger (ex to allow for larger deals and future larger business deals, etc.) Minimal "friction" of processes (avoid cramped work areas, analyze worker movement paths) Implement by having car fab stations that feed into the next process, while gaps between sub processes are a highway for supply of parts and hardware sourced from outside the river to the needed station. Processes are uni-directional, and scales linearly by adding extra rows to the process Good for high volume production and scalability A single car operation unit requires a weld table for the frame, and also space to assemble parts to the frame. Processes are self contained, and scales squarely Good for single-low volume production Use "River" processes to spread workers to their specialized station and supply lower volume components in a "Matrix" fashion Need to ensure that rates are feasibly distributed, so rivers may fork or converge to increase or decrease rates at a node. This prevents clogging or wasted worker time. Determine listed subprocess granularity and which processes to link Link process order in time Determine which processes will be river and which will be matrix Determine needed river forks to maintain consistent rates
Will be parallel batch process, determine vehicles simultaneous to reach production targets in time. initial post construction ✅ 2026-03-14 Determine how N determines workspace/warehouse size requirement Determine work space needed for various scenarios (operation diameter): Determine Allocated fraction of people for each process (make process scale with ) Consider the relationship between adding people to work (increases cost by the hour, shortens overall time, required space, but allows for greater parallelization given the proper setup) and removing people to work (decreases labour cost by hour, increases overall time, requires less space, less parallelization capability.) Min space Welding Lathe Component post processing Composite work Car assembly Arrange processes spatially, reference process timing graph Create parametrically limited production diagram: grid of simultaneous vehicles being worked on by people each. Determine for current capacity and show that can grow and the space will accommodate that Determine which processes are general and which are duplicated per unit Determine size and additional process spec of areas external to manufacturing in warehouse (admin, shipping, bulk storage etc.) Place these things with consideration to nearby things Specify QA process Dual Specs (Warehouse and client side spec) Specify spare parts storage and supply Specify maintenance area process Specify fueling process Summary on how this Manu process ensures "long-term profitability with minimal downtime and overhead operating costs.**" Demonstrate capability to execute the "additional opportunity ... to develop part of the property into UTV trails." Show that we have space and man power allocation room to store necessary tools and equipment Have the knowledge and expertise to carry out the work in a safe, quality-first, and efficient manner
Will be parallel batch process, determine vehicles simultaneous to reach production targets in time. initial post constructionLorem Ipsum Component Fabrication ↓ Subsystem Assembly ↓ Finishing & Coating ↓ Final Vehicle Assembly ↓ Test & DeliveryLorem IpsumLorem IpsumLorem IpsumLorem IpsumLorem IpsumLorem IpsumLorem IpsumLorem Ipsum
item.guid: Baja/Projects/Business Presentation Manufacturing plan/Manufacturing Implementation Plan.md
